prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a statistical model using an estimated historical volatility, assuming a normal distribution of returns. Given the absence of real-time market data beyond my last training update, an estimated average daily volatility of 1.2% for a large-cap stock like TMO was used. This daily volatility was then scaled by the square root of the number of days (sqrt(6) ≈ 2.45) to project the volatility over a 6-day period, resulting in an approximate 6-day absolute standard deviation of $15.30. The point estimate is assumed to be the current price ($520.60), reflecting a neutral short-term expectation. Confidence intervals are calculated using standard z-scores corresponding to the desired confidence levels (0.674 for 50%, 1.282 for 80%, and 1.645 for 90%) applied to this projected 6-day standard deviation around the point estimate.
